course_description:
Topological spaces: compactness, continuity, separation axioms, Baire category theorem. Classical linear functional analysis: Banach and Hilbert spaces, linear functionals, L^p spaces (as completions), Riesz representation theorem, uniform boundedness principle, dual spaces, weak convergence, Hahn-Banach theorem, bounded operators, open mapping theorem, spectrum and resolvent.

The course aims to introduce students to the basic and fundamentalﾠconcepts in point-set Topology and Functional Analysis.

During the first part of the course (4 weeks) we will study abstract topological spaces, introducing basic notions such as compactness, continuity, separation axioms, locally compact spaces. We will also cover important theorems such as Tychonoff's theorem, Urysohn's lemma and the Baire category theorem. Throughout we will be relying on metric spaces as fundamental examples.

The rest ﾠof the course will be focused on studying Functional Analysis. ﾠMore precisely, the central objects of study are Banach spaces: ﾠvector spaces (not necessarily of finite dimension) endowed with a complete norm. Special cases include Hilbert spaces, when the norm comes from an inner product.

In the School of Mathematics and Physics we are committed to creating an inclusive and empowering learning environment for all students. We value and respect the diverse range of experiences our students bring to their education, and we believe that this diversity is crucial for fostering a rich culture of knowledge sharing and meaningful exploration. We hold both students and staff accountable for actively contributing to the establishment of a respectful and supportive learning environment.

Bullying, harassment, and discrimination in any form are strictly against our principles and against UQ Policy, and will not be tolerated. We have developed a suite of resources ﾠ to assist you in recognising, reporting, and addressing such behaviour. If you have any concerns about your experience in this course, we encourage you to tell a member of the course teaching team, or alternatively contact an SMP Classroom Inclusivity Champion (see Blackboard for contact details). Our Inclusivity Champions are here to listen, to understand your concerns, and to explore potential actions that can be taken to resolve them. Your well-being and a positive learning atmosphere are of utmost importance to us.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the definition and fundamental properties of metric spaces, including the ideas of convergence, continuity, completeness, compactness and connectedness, and apply them in different contexts;
Understand the concept of topological spaces, their properties, and their relation to metric spaces.
Understand normed and Hilbert spaces, their properties, and their relation to metric and topological spaces.
Prove simple results about metric, topological, normed and Hilbert spaces;
Generalise basic concepts of geometry and linear algebra to infinite dimensional spaces;

assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your assignment through Blackboard in a PDF format
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See ADDITIONAL ASSESSMENT INFORMATION for further extension information relating to this assessment item.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item then the listed penalty will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point any submission will not receive any marks unless an extension has been approved. Each 24-hour block is recorded from the time the submission is due.
See ADDITIONAL ASSESSMENT INFORMATION for further extension information relating to this assessment item.
